mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint growth periods yield negative median forward returns or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with industrial production changes over a 5-year sample with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent annually while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, negative real yields trigger equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive during high-inflation low-rate periods or the signal loses predictive power after 2014 with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payrolls above 200k coincide with negative median forward returns in >55% of observations over the backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth below 1.5 percent combined with declining retail sales indicates a demand contraction that reduces earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with a p-value below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the index of industrial production signal weakening manufacturing activity and lower corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of declining industrial production months or Rank IC stays below 0.01.
